title: Consultation: Draft technical document guidelines for Canadian drinking water quality - Antimony - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation: Draft technical document guidelines for Canadian drinking water quality - Antimony

## Current status: Closed
Opened on December 24, 2022, and will close to new input on March 8, 2023.
Health Canada is updating its guidance document on antimony in drinking water. We're looking for comments from regulatory authorities, decision makers and the public on the draft document .

## Join in: How to participate
Send an email to water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca with your ideas or comments to make yourself heard.
Send a letter with your ideas and input to the address in contact information below.

## Who is the focus of this consultation
We invite input from:
- federal, provincial and territorial regulatory and health protection agencies
- industry stakeholders
- academic communities
- non-governmental organizations
- members of the general public

## Key questions for discussion
We're looking for comments on these topics:
- the proposed guidelines
- the approach used for their development
- the potential impacts of implementation
We will use the information we gather from this consultation to prepare and update the Guidelines for Canadian drinking water quality: Guideline technical document – Antimony.

### Water and Air Quality Bureau
Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave West Address Locator 4903B Ottawa ON K1A 0K9
Email: water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca
